scores.materiality_reasoning: Political speech before the assembly is a concrete legal right with direct material power in a democracy.
scores.generality_reasoning: The right to speak (isegoria) is a foundational societal axis for Athenian citizenship.
scores.persistence_reasoning: The loss of rights (atimia) for shield-deserters was a permanent lifelong legal standing.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Citizenship is a legal birth-status, though here modified by a permanent legal conviction.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Lysias is prosecuting/defending in a contemporary Athenian court regarding current laws.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Verbatim cites a specific past trial (Lysitheus vs Theomnestus) based on this statute.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Speaking rights are a consequence of status, not the definition of a soldier.

scores.materiality_reasoning: A penalty of 500 drachmae is a tangible monetary transfer.
scores.generality_reasoning: Relates to a specific recurring legal action for slander (dike kakegorias).
scores.persistence_reasoning: Applies to the duration of a single trial and its one-time payout.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Citizenship is a hard-to-change legal birth-status.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: The author discusses a law currently in force in his own city.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: The verbatim quotes the specific legal penalty (500 drachmae) as an established fact.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Protection from slander is not part of the definition of citizenship.

scores.materiality_reasoning: Legal redress for physical injury (doubled penalty) is a tangible bodily and monetary protection.
scores.generality_reasoning: A specific legal protection regarding physical assault damages.
scores.persistence_reasoning: Holds as long as the individual remains in the status of a servant.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Slaves/servants held a structural legal status requiring manumission to change.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Lysias cites 'ancient laws' (of Solon) still treated as valid statute in his time.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Verbatim provides a literal definition and statutory instruction from the law.
scores.tautology_reasoning: The amount of redress for injury is not part of the definition of being a 'varlet'.

scores.materiality_reasoning: The right to set interest rates is a direct entitlement to wealth accumulation.
scores.generality_reasoning: Covers the broad economic category of lending and capital return.
scores.persistence_reasoning: Holds for the duration of one's participation in the lending market.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Being a lender/wealthy person is a changeable but durable economic identity.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Refers to contemporary Athenian financial law cited in court.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: The verbatim quotes a specific legal provision ('Money shall be placed out...').
scores.tautology_reasoning: Being a lender implies having capital, but not necessarily the legal right to unregulated interest.

scores.materiality_reasoning: Confinement in stocks is a direct physical/bodily restraint.
scores.generality_reasoning: A specific recurring court-ordered penalty.
scores.persistence_reasoning: The resource (bodily freedom) is lost for a brief five-day episode.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Applicable to the role-bound status of a convicted defendant.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Refers to a law of Solon still active in the author's present.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Verbatim cites the law's specific procedural instruction.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Citizenship does not define current innocence or freedom from types of punishment.

scores.materiality_reasoning: Recovery of equipment allowances involves specific state funds/debts.
scores.generality_reasoning: Tied to a specific post-war decree (return of the democracy, 403 BC).
scores.persistence_reasoning: Relates to a single historical settlement of accounts.
scores.immutability_reasoning: The group is defined by their past episodic service in the cavalry under the Thirty.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Author is describing a decree passed shortly before the trial in his lifetime.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Reports on a specific vote and official process (recovering allowances).
scores.tautology_reasoning: Being a cavalryman does not inherently define whether you owe a refund to the state.

scores.materiality_reasoning: Securing public office leads to direct political and potential material power.
scores.generality_reasoning: Access to public office is a major category of civic life.
scores.persistence_reasoning: The outcome of a dokimasia determines a year-long (or career-long) right to serve.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Magistracy candidates are an episodic group defined by their pursuit of office.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: The author is undergoing this exact contemporary legal procedure.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Describes the factual legal 'right to render an account' in a scrutiny.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Being a citizen candidate does not mean you have already passed the scrutiny.

scores.materiality_reasoning: A dowry of thirty minae is a substantial tangible wealth transfer.
scores.generality_reasoning: Dowry is a foundation of family property and marriage in this society.
scores.persistence_reasoning: The dowry holds for the duration of the marriage or its dissolution.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Daughters are biologically defined members of a lineage.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Refers to recent family events within the author’s own society.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Reports on personal actions taken according to standard social facts.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Being a daughter does not inherently mean one possesses a dowry.

scores.materiality_reasoning: Financial support (30 drachmae) for service is a tangible cash resource.
scores.generality_reasoning: A specific social expectation tied to military expenses.
scores.persistence_reasoning: Resource is bounded to the duration of a specific military service period.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Poverty/wealth is a durable socio-economic identity.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Describes a social convention active in the author's contemporary Athens.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Report of actual behavior based on an accepted social norm ('ought to provide').
scores.tautology_reasoning: Being poor is defined by lack of money, not by the receipt of state-based aid.

scores.materiality_reasoning: The right to speak before the people is a major political resource.
scores.generality_reasoning: Public speaking is a foundational political right for citizens.
scores.persistence_reasoning: This restriction is tied to the life stage of youth.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Age is a biologically fixed status at a given time.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Deals with the author's own experience in the contemporary assembly.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Factual observation of social 'annoyance' indicating a normative restriction.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Being minor/young does not logically entail not speaking in every culture.

scores.materiality_reasoning: Protection from disenfranchisement preserves the material benefits of citizenship.
scores.generality_reasoning: Citizen status is a broad category spanning most rights.
scores.persistence_reasoning: Citizenship is a lifelong, structural status.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Citizenship is a hard-to-change legal birth-status.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Describes the typical behavior of contemporary Athenian juries.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Report of a common court practice ('we find... you take pity').
scores.tautology_reasoning: Protection from losing a status is not part of the status's definition.

scores.materiality_reasoning: Special levies (eisphora) and liturgies are tangible financial extractions.
scores.generality_reasoning: Liturgy obligations are a major recurring structural axis for the wealthy.
scores.persistence_reasoning: The obligation persists as long as the member remains within a wealth bracket.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Wealth is a durable identity but changeable through loss of property.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Describes the contemporary Athenian liturgical system.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Cites the necessity to perform public services as a factual legal requirement.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Wealth does not define the obligation to perform specific state services.

scores.materiality_reasoning: Right to hold office provides direct material and social power.
scores.generality_reasoning: Eligibility for magistracies is a broad class of citizen rights.
scores.persistence_reasoning: Eligibility is a lifelong/long-term status for citizens.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Citizenship is legal birth-status.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Discusses contemporary Athenian political institutions.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Reported fact of the defendant having held many magistracies.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Being a citizen does not define the right to hold office in every society.

scores.materiality_reasoning: Protection from summary punishment is a direct bodily entitlement.
scores.generality_reasoning: The right to a trial is a foundational legal personhood axis.
scores.persistence_reasoning: Tied to the specific episodic life-stage of being a defendant.
scores.immutability_reasoning: A defendant is a role-bound episodic group.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Author is speaking in an actual contemporary trial.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Refers to the defendant 'submitting to justice' as an available legal mechanism.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Being a citizen does not define the specific procedural right to trial.

scores.materiality_reasoning: Being recorded as a citizen grants all the material rights of the state.
scores.generality_reasoning: Registration is the process for a major right (citizenship).
scores.persistence_reasoning: Once registered, citizenship was typically lifelong and hereditary.
scores.immutability_reasoning: The 'nine thousand' were an episodic group defined by a specific political decree.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Refers to a recent historical event in Athenian politics.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Reports on the Registrar's factual actions and the decree's terms.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Being one of the 'townsmen' does not give you citizenship by definition.

scores.materiality_reasoning: The right to inhabit the city and avoid exile is a foundational bodily/material resource.
scores.generality_reasoning: Residency and civic belonging are foundational societal axes.
scores.persistence_reasoning: Citizenship and its accompanying right of residency are lifelong.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Citizenship is structural birth-status.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: The speaker is addressing a contemporary Athenian jury to avoid exile.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Rhetorical appeal combined with the factual consequence of losing status.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Citizen status does not define the physical location of residency.

scores.materiality_reasoning: Leniency in sentencing directly affects a defendant's freedom or wealth.
scores.generality_reasoning: A specific legal norm applied during trials.
scores.persistence_reasoning: Affects the outcome of a single trial episode.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Age is a biologically fixed status.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Refers to contemporary judicial norms and interpersonal appeals in court.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Verbatim is an active rhetorical appeal/exhortation for pity.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Being elderly does not define the type of legal outcome one receives.

scores.materiality_reasoning: Death is the ultimate material/bodily consequence of a rule.
scores.generality_reasoning: A specific limit (50 measures) on a professional trade practice.
scores.persistence_reasoning: The professional restriction holds as long as one is an active corn-dealer.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Resident alien (metic) is a durable identity; occupation is a chosen training.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: The author is conducting an actual cross-examination in a contemporary trial.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: The verbatim cites the specific legal limit (50 measures) as a known law.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Being an alien does not define the quantity of grain one can buy.

scores.materiality_reasoning: Restricting profit margin (one obol) is a direct material economic limitation.
scores.generality_reasoning: Hyper-specific limit for a single sector (grain retail).
scores.persistence_reasoning: Holds as long as the individual remains in the corn-dealer occupation.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Occupational identity is durable but acquired through practice/training.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Part of a contemporary Athenian legal case about market regulation.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Cites the specific legal obligation ('add no more than an obol').
scores.tautology_reasoning: A dealer is a seller; the specific profit cap is external to that definition.

scores.materiality_reasoning: Prohibiting hoarding (holding in store) restricts physical stock management for profit.
scores.generality_reasoning: Specific commercial regulation in the grain market.
scores.persistence_reasoning: Holds as long as one is practicing the occupation of dealer.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Occupation is a durable social identity.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Author is prosecuting based on current Athenian market laws.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Discussion of specific orders vs. advice regarding legal and illegal acts.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Being a dealer does not inherently define whether one hoards or sells.
